summary.title: PERVO - Privacy-Enhanced Resort and Vacancy Online
summary.description: DFD for the PERVO system managing bookings and logistics of a luxury resort, including AI-based facial recognition for access control.

data.name: On-Site / External Principals
data.description: Physical guests, on-site staff, cameras and the external IdP

data.name: Internal Service Layer
data.description: Trusted internal microservices

data.name: AI Subsystem Boundary
data.description: Isolated AI facial recognition and media processing

data.name: Database Layer
data.description: Isolated database tier — no direct external access

data.name: Guest
data.description: Resort guest. Books stay online (pre-arrival) and is physically present on-site. Registered under pseudonym in operational systems.

data.name: Staff
data.description: Resort staff operating the system on-site. Accesses only pseudonymized data and booking information. Never sees guest PII.

data.name: Surveillance Cameras
data.description: Physical on-site IP cameras streaming video feed to the AI facial recognition service for access control and intruder detection.

data.name: External IdP
data.description: External identity provider. Issues JWTs for authenticated staff and guests. Not under direct system control.

threats.title: T8 - Credential theft yields impersonated JWT
threats.description: An attacker steals staff or admin credentials and authenticates to the external IdP, receiving a valid JWT that grants system access under a spoofed identity. Enables impersonation of legitimate users.
threats.mitigation: Enforce MFA on IdP. Validate JWT audience and scope claims. Monitor for anomalous login locations.

data.name: Booking API
data.description: Handles online pre-arrival bookings from guests and on-site staff requests. Authenticates via External IdP. Entry point exposed to external network for pre-arrival bookings.

threats.title: T1 - Spoofing of staff identity via stolen credentials
threats.description: Attacker uses stolen staff credentials (e.g., from an unattended on-site terminal) to spoof a legitimate staff identity and access the Booking API with staff-level privileges.
threats.mitigation: Enforce MFA. Implement session binding to device. Auto-lock terminals after inactivity (SR2).

threats.title: T2 - SQL injection via booking request fields
threats.description: Attacker submits crafted input in booking request fields that is passed unsanitised to the database layer, enabling modification or exfiltration of booking and guest records.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterised queries / ORM. Input validation. Apply SR6 (PII access control).

threats.title: T3 - Session hijacking at on-site terminal
threats.description: An adversary physically accesses an unattended authenticated staff terminal and hijacks the active session, gaining access to booking data and guest pseudonyms without re-authentication.
threats.mitigation: Short session timeout (SR2). Screen lock after inactivity. Physical access controls on staff terminals.

threats.title: T4 - DDoS disrupts booking and on-site access
threats.description: A volumetric DDoS attack floods the Booking API, making it unavailable. Since access control depends on system availability (AI matching), this may also disrupt physical access management at the resort.
threats.mitigation: Rate limiting. CDN/WAF with DDoS mitigation. Offline fallback access control procedure.

data.name: Pseudonymization Service
data.description: Maps real guest PII to pseudonymous identifiers. The only internal service authorised to bridge the PII DB and Pseudonymized DB. Processes new guest registrations and manages the pseudonym lifecycle.

threats.title: T5 - Broken access control allows privilege escalation to PII
threats.description: A staff user with access to the Pseudonymization Service exploits a missing authorisation check to invoke identity resolution functions, obtaining real PII records behind guest pseudonyms.
threats.mitigation: Enforce role-based access control (SR3). Staff roles must be denied PII-resolution endpoints. Regular access control audits.

data.name: Media Linker Service
data.description: Controlled service that links media subjects (AI-identified faces) to pseudonymous identities for operational purposes. Access strictly scoped and audited.

data.name: Audit Logging Service
data.description: Receives audit events from all internal services. Write-only from service perspective. Logs stored separately and protected from modification.

data.name: AI Facial Recognition Service
data.description: Receives video streams from on-site surveillance cameras. Matches faces against registered guests and staff in the biometric/pseudonymized database for access control and intruder detection. Returns match confirmation only — never exposes PII.

threats.title: T9 (AI) - Manipulated video feed bypasses facial recognition
threats.description: An attacker (e.g., sophisticated intruder or insider) injects a manipulated or pre-recorded video feed into the camera stream, causing the AI to match an authorised guest/staff face and grant physical access to an unauthorised individual.
threats.mitigation: Authenticate camera feeds (signed video streams). Anomaly detection on feed metadata. SR4 (confidence threshold enforcement).

threats.title: T10 (AI) - AI model extraction exposes biometric identities
threats.description: An adversary with API access systematically queries the AI facial recognition service with crafted inputs to extract the underlying model via model-stealing attacks, subsequently reconstructing biometric embeddings that can be linked to real guest identities.
threats.mitigation: Rate-limit and monitor AI API queries. SR5 (restrict model access to admins only). Differential privacy on model outputs.

data.name: PII Database
data.description: Stores real guest identities, billing information, and personal data. Accessible only by the Pseudonymization Service. Never directly queried by staff-facing services.

threats.title: T6 - SQL injection reveals full guest PII
threats.description: A successful SQL injection attack against the PII database (via a compromised or poorly sanitised internal service) exposes real names, billing information, and personal data of all registered guests.
threats.mitigation: Parameterised queries. Strict network-level access control to PII DB. SR6 (encryption of data in transit between services and PII DB).

data.name: Pseudonymized Database
data.description: Stores operational records (bookings, food preferences, special requests) linked to pseudonymous identifiers. Accessible by Booking API and AI matching service (read-only for match).

threats.title: T7 - Unauthorized modification of guest preferences
threats.description: A staff member or compromised service account modifies guest preference or booking records without authorisation, potentially disrupting personalised services or exposing operational information about specific pseudonymous guests.
threats.mitigation: Role-based access control (SR3): staff read-only on pseudonymized DB. Audit all write operations (SR8).

data.name: Media Database
data.description: Stores surveillance recordings and processed video data. Linked to pseudonymous identifiers, not PII directly. Access strictly controlled and audited.

threats.title: T12 - Unauthorized access to surveillance footage
threats.description: An attacker or insider with elevated privileges accesses the media database directly, retrieving surveillance footage that reveals the presence, activities, and interactions of high-profile guests.
threats.mitigation: Strict access control on media DB. Encrypt stored recordings. Audit all access. Separate media DB credentials from other services.

data.name: Audit Log Store
data.description: Write-only, append-only store for all system audit events. Physically and logically separated from operational databases.

threats.title: T11 - Log tampering to cover unauthorized access
threats.description: An attacker or malicious insider modifies or deletes audit log entries to erase evidence of unauthorised data access or privilege escalation, enabling undetected breach of guest privacy.
threats.mitigation: Append-only log store with cryptographic signing. SR8 (encrypted and signed audit trail). Offsite log replication.

data.name: Online booking request (HTTPS/TLS)
data.description: Guest submits booking online prior to arrival. Encrypted channel.

data.name: On-site staff request (internal network)
data.description: Staff operates the system from on-site terminals on the internal resort network.

data.name: Auth request
data.description: Booking API forwards authentication request to external IdP.

data.name: JWT token
data.description: External IdP returns signed JWT upon successful authentication.

data.name: New guest registration (PII)
data.description: Booking API forwards guest PII to Pseudonymization Service for registration and pseudonym generation.

data.name: Store PII
data.description: Pseudonymization Service writes real guest PII to the PII database.

data.name: Store pseudonym + preferences
data.description: Pseudonymization Service writes pseudonymous record and operational data to the Pseudonymized Database.

data.name: Read/write booking data
data.description: Booking API reads and updates booking records using pseudonymous guest identifiers.

data.name: Video feed (on-site network)
data.description: Surveillance cameras stream video over the secured on-site internal network to the AI Facial Recognition Service.

data.name: Store recordings + face embeddings
data.description: AI service stores processed video recordings and anonymised face embeddings in the Media Database.

data.name: Match lookup (pseudonym only)
data.description: AI service queries pseudonymized DB to confirm whether a detected face matches a registered guest or staff member. Returns match/no-match only.

data.name: Controlled identity resolution
data.description: Media Linker Service resolves pseudonymous media subject to real identity only under strictly authorised circumstances (e.g., law enforcement request). Fully audited.

data.name: Audit events
data.description: All access and modification events sent to Audit Logging Service.

data.name: AI audit events
data.description: AI recognition events, match results, and access decisions logged.

data.name: Write audit log (append-only)
data.description: Audit Logging Service writes signed, encrypted entries to the append-only Audit Log Store.
